message.text: Swig template engine autoescape is explicitly disabled (autoescape: false). This globally disables HTML entity encoding for all template variables rendered with {{ }}, exposing every view to reflected and stored XSS. The commented-out fix confirms this is intentional for demonstration but represents a critical misconfiguration.

Stored XSS via memo rendering. User-submitted memos are rendered through marked() with autoescape disabled: {{ marked(doc.memo) }}. The marked library's sanitize option is deprecated and unreliable. An attacker can store malicious JavaScript in a memo that executes in every user's browser who views the memos page.

Reflected XSS in profile page. Multiple user-controlled fields (lastName, ssn, dob, bankAcc, bankRouting, address, website, updateError) are rendered unescaped in the profile template via {{ variable }} with autoescape disabled. An attacker who controls these database values (e.g., via profile update) can inject scripts that execute when the profile page is rendered.

Reflected XSS in login page. The loginError and userName variables are rendered unescaped via {{loginError}} and {{userName}}. If the server reflects user input (e.g., a bad username) into these variables without sanitization, an attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript.

Reflected XSS in signup page. Multiple error messages and form re-population values (userNameError, firstNameError, lastNameError, passwordError, verifyError, emailError, userName, firstName, lastName) are rendered without escaping. If the server echoes user input in these variables, script injection is possible.

Stored XSS in layout navigation bar. The {{firstName}} and {{lastName}} variables are rendered unescaped in the layout template's navigation header. Since layout.html is the base template for all authenticated pages, a user who sets their name to a malicious script payload will have it executed on every page load for their session.

Unescaped rendering of environmentalScripts in layout and login templates. The {{script}} variable inside the loop is rendered without escaping. If the environmentalScripts array is populated from an untrusted source or can be influenced by an attacker, arbitrary script injection is possible.

Stored XSS in benefits admin page. User-controlled fields (user.firstName, user.lastName, user.benefitStartDate) are rendered unescaped in the benefits table. A user who sets their name to a malicious payload will have it execute in the admin's browser when they view the benefits page.

Stored XSS in allocations page. The {{allocation.firstName}} and {{allocation.lastName}} values are rendered without escaping. An attacker who modifies their profile name to include script tags will have the payload execute when any user views the allocations page.

Reflected XSS in error template. The {{error}} variable is rendered without escaping in error-template.html. If the error message contains user-controlled input (e.g., from a URL path or parameter that triggers an error), an attacker can inject arbitrary scripts.

Swig 1.4.2 is an unmaintained template engine (last updated 2014, archived). It has known security issues and receives no patches. The project should migrate to a maintained template engine such as Nunjucks, EJS, or Handlebars.
